ucs.title: [LLM] TG-1314 abuse of enterprise software deployment system to push tooling via compromised domain account
ucs.description: Hunts TG-1314's signature chain: a stolen domain credential authenticates to remote-access infrastructure, then abuses an enterprise software deployment platform (Altiris/SCCM/LANDesk-class) to fan out cmd.exe-driven payloads to endpoints over SMB. Per Dell SecureWorks reporting on TG-1314 (G0028), the operators pivoted from a legitimately compromised account into the victim's own deployment server and used it as a trusted distribution channel — a behavior that distinguishes TG-1314 from generic credential-theft crews.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("AeXNSAgent.exe","AeXNSClient.exe","CcmExec.exe","smstsmgr.exe","WUAHandler.exe","TPMC.exe","sdclient.exe","landesk*")) AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count as smb_writes values(Filesystem.file_path) as written_files from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\ADMIN$\\*","*\\C$\\Windows\\*","*\\Altiris\\*","*\\ccmcache\\*") AND Filesystem.action=created by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user] | join type=inner user [| tstats summariesonly=true count as logon_count values(Authentication.src) as logon_src from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action=success AND Authentication.authentication_method IN ("NTLM","Kerberos") AND Authentication.user_category="domain" by Authentication.user] | where logon_count>=3 AND smb_writes>=1 | eval risk_score=logon_count+(smb_writes*5) | sort - risk_score | table firstTime dest user parent_process_name process_name cmdline written_files logon_src risk_score
ucs.kql:
let deploymentParents = dynamic(["AeXNSAgent.exe","AeXNSClient.exe","CcmExec.exe","smstsmgr.exe","WUAHandler.exe","TPMC.exe","sdclient.exe","LANDeskAgent.exe"]);
let suspiciousChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe"]);
let windowStart = ago(7d);
let deploymentExec = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > windowStart
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (deploymentParents)
| where FileName in~ (suspiciousChildren)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("\\\\","-enc","IEX","DownloadString","Invoke-","net use","copy ","xcopy","certutil","bitsadmin")
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName=InitiatingProcessAccountName, AccountDomain=InitiatingProcessAccountDomain, ParentImage=InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine;
let smbStaging = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > windowStart
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
| where FolderPath has_any ("\\Windows\\System32\\","\\Windows\\Temp\\","\\ProgramData\\","\\ccmcache\\","\\Altiris\\")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("System","svchost.exe") or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (deploymentParents)
| project SmbTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, WrittenFile=FileName, FolderPath, RemoteIP=InitiatingProcessRemoteIP;
let interactiveLogons = IdentityLogonEvents
| where Timestamp > windowStart
| where ActionType == "LogonSuccess"
| where LogonType in ("Interactive","RemoteInteractive","Network")
| where Protocol in ("Kerberos","NTLM")
| summarize LogonCount=count(), SrcIPs=make_set(IPAddress,10), FirstLogon=min(Timestamp) by AccountName=tolower(AccountName), AccountDomain=tolower(AccountDomain), DeviceName;
deploymentExec
| join kind=inner (smbStaging) on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between ((SmbTime - 30m) .. (SmbTime + 30m))
| join kind=inner (interactiveLogons) on $left.DeviceName == $right.DeviceName
| where AccountName == tolower(AccountName1)
| extend AttackChain = strcat("DomainAcct(", AccountName, ") -> SMBStage(", WrittenFile, ") -> DeployTool(", ParentImage, ") -> Exec(", FileName, ")")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain, ParentImage, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, WrittenFile, FolderPath, SrcIPs, LogonCount, AttackChain
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: TG-1314 is publicly characterized by exactly three things: (1) using legitimate domain credentials rather than malware-delivered access, (2) pivoting through the victim's own remote-access plumbing, and (3) co-opting the enterprise software-deployment server to push tooling laterally over admin shares — a tradecraft mix that ordinary credential-stuffing crews and commodity ransomware affiliates do
